signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 CPI inflation rose to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's March 2026 CPI inflation rose to 2.2% YoY (from 2.0% in Feb), below 2.3-2.4% market consensus, driven by oil but curbed by government measures like fuel caps | YES | HIGH confidence** [1][3]

This data, released around early April 2026, confirms contained price pressures despite global oil spikes, with core CPI at 2.2% YoY (down from 2.3%) and monthly CPI at 0.3% (vs 0.6% expected);
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?
